task: Write a Python function max_index(nums) returning the index of the largest value in the list. When the largest value appears more than once, return the index of its last occurrence, not the first. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution correctly identifies the index of the last occurrence of the maximum value, but only handles the empty list edge case.
trace: To solve this problem, I first checked for the edge case where the input list is empty and raised a `ValueError` to handle it gracefully. Then, I utilized Python's slicing feature to reverse the list and find the index of the maximum value in the reversed list. By subtracting this index from the length of the original list minus one, I ensured that if the largest value appears more than once, the function returns the index of its last occurrence. This approach is concise and leverages Python's powerful list manipulation capabilities to achieve the desired functionality efficiently.
